Description: In collaboration with Pennrose, REACH Riverside’s housing developer partner, this strategy delivers high-quality, mixed-income rental housing through multi-phase apartment complex development (Imani Village, which is being built over the course of nine phases). The development prioritizes legacy Riverside residents, offering them first right to occupancy. By intentionally mixing deeply subsidized, affordable, and market-rate units, this approach creates economically integrated communities with the aim of breaking cycles of concentrated poverty, while ensuring that existing Riverside households can remain in the neighborhood.
Immediate Outcomes: Increased access to affordable rental housing
Intermediate Outcomes: Increase in families moving into affordable rental housing
Long-term Outcomes:
Increased legacy resident retention
Increased housing stability

Activities: Real Estate Strategy to Obtain Racial Equity (RESTORE) program
Description: Leveraging funding from a Choice Neighborhood Implementation grant (in addition to a variety of other ongoing funding sources) and a wide range of partnerships across Riverside, this strategy reclaims underutilized and blighted property across Riverside, converting land into community-serving assets. This comprehensive redevelopment encompasses new community facilities, single-family housing development, and critical community resources including food retail, food production infrastructure, community gathering spaces, and mixed-use facilities. By RESTORING vacant lots and deteriorating buildings into vibrant community spaces and businesses, this strategy improves the physical environment while creating wealth-building opportunities for residents through community business ownership and employment.
Output:
Increased financial investment in communal assets
Property RESTORED
Immediate Outcomes:
Increased access to wealth creation opportunities
Increased acreage RESTORED
Intermediate Outcomes:
Community businesses created and funded
Increased utilization of community assets

Description: This strategy creates pathways to homeownership and generational wealth by developing for-sale housing units through multiple development models. The Community Land Trust model ensures permanent affordability by separating land ownership from home ownership, allowing families to build equity through home appreciation while keeping housing costs accessible for future generations. Combined with homeownership preparation services including credit counseling, financial literacy, and down payment assistance navigation, this strategy removes traditional barriers to homeownership for low- and moderate-income families while prioritizing legacy residents and directly countering displacement pressures.
Output:
Home ownership units built
Community Land Trust formed
Referrals to home ownership preparation program
Immediate Outcomes:
Increased access to purchasing permanently affordable housing
Increased home ownership skills
Intermediate Outcomes:
Increased home ownership readiness
Increase in families purchasing homes
Long-term Outcomes:
Increased housing stability
Increased legacy resident retention
Generational wealth creation through home ownership

Description: This strategy provides comprehensive wrap-around services to ensure housing stability across Riverside. Families receive individualized support, including financial literacy training; rental, utility and mortgage assistance; and home ownership preparation programs. Data from family strengths and needs assessments informs ongoing service navigation support and connections with tailored resources aligned with family goals. This holistic approach addresses the multifaceted barriers to housing stability—from managing rental delinquency to improving the quality of neighborhood homes—while preparing families for the transition from renting to homeownership and building the financial acumen necessary for long-term stability and wealth creation.

Description:
This strategy provides rigorous academic programming for students from kindergarten through high school graduation through EastSide Charter School (K-8th grade) and Colonial School District (K-12th grade) schools. EastSide Charter School offers lower school (K-3rd grade), upper school (4th-8th grade), honors programming, and STEM-focused instruction to build toward college and career readiness. For high school students and specialized services, the strategy relies on partnership with public high schools as part of Colonial School District. 

Supplementing core academics, student navigators work directly with chronically absent or academically struggling students, offering personalized support to address barriers to learning and build confidence through regular meetings and goal-setting, while family navigators work alongside families to help them navigate challenges, identify opportunities, and connect with community resources through personalized success plans. Outside of school hours, students have access to additional services and support through The Warehouse (see Out of School Time strategy).

Students enrolled
Tutoring, mentorship, and academic support received
Student navigator support received

Students taking the PSAT, SAT, and/or ACT exams
Long-term Outcomes:
Increased resilience and protective factors among youth and young adults
Increased K-12 academic achievement
Increased high school graduation
Increased post-secondary achievement

Description:
This comprehensive strategy provides academically enriching, developmentally appropriate programming during non-school hours for children, youth and young adults. Before and after care serves elementary-aged children, while summer camp programming serves 5-12 year olds, including a range of academic enrichment, sports and recreation, performance and visual arts, social-emotional learning, field trips, and nutritious meals. 

Youth and young adults (ages 13-24) from all area middle and high schools, meanwhile, have access to a variety of programs (Recreation, Education, Arts, Careers, and Health) during afternoon and evening hours at The Warehouse. The Warehouse programming aims to support youth and young adults' growth by providing safe, engaging, and development-focused opportunities that equip youth with the skills, confidence, and connections needed to succeed academically, socially, and professionally, while fostering a supportive community where they can explore their interests, build meaningful relationships, and access comprehensive enrichment experiences that nurture personal development, strengthen life skills, and prepare them for future education and career pathways.

Description: This strategy ensures residents have access to comprehensive, affordable physical and mental health services in addition to needed community resources. Health services are embedded in community facilities, bringing specialized care directly into the community and eliminating transportation and access barriers that often prevent families from seeking care. Community Events, such as an annual Holiday Toy and Coat Drive, connect community members with needed resources. Building on this, food access programs address food insecurity through a free pantry, community refrigerators, urban agriculture, and snack distribution strategically placed at key venues such as The Warehouse. Additionally at The Warehouse, mentoring supports and mental health services support youth and young adults in reaching their full potential.

Strategy: Building a Vibrant, Socially Cohesive Community
Description: This strategy weaves together multiple initiatives that strengthen social bonds, increase community participation, and build collective efficacy across the neighborhood. Community events including seasonal celebrations, movie screenings, holiday initiatives, and ceremonial gatherings create regular opportunities for celebration and belonging. Community ambassadors promote events and strengthen neighbor-to-neighbor connections. Volunteer management mobilizes community members and external supporters to staff food distribution, events, and special projects, building a culture of civic engagement. Community tours provide transparent visibility into revitalization efforts, building trust and investment. At The Warehouse, youth and young adults build relationships and community connectivity through sports and recreation, arts programming, health education, and educational workshops. Description:
This strategy provides intensive, ongoing support to help residents, including youth and young adults, achieve financial stability and build wealth. For youth and young adults, RISE offers eight weeks of durable skills training and personal/career development followed by a four-week work-related experience or certification, depending on participant needs and goals. RISE programs for both in-school and out-of-school youth integrate financial literacy as a core component of durable skills training, ensuring young people develop money management capabilities alongside employment readiness. 

Through EMPOWER, participants complete self-sufficiency assessments at regular intervals to track progress across multiple dimensions of financial wellbeing, while developing and working toward individualized service plans that address their specific goals and barriers. One-on-one financial coaching focuses on improving credit scores, reducing personal debt, increasing savings, and working toward asset goals including motor vehicle and home purchases. The strategy combines financial education workshops on topics like budgeting, banking, and credit with practical assistance navigating challenges like rental delinquency, utility arrears, and employment barriers.

Immediate Outcomes:
Increased access to financial education workshops
Increased access to legal assistance
Increased financial management knowledge
Intermediate Outcomes:
Increased financial planning
Increased income, savings, assets, and/or decreased debt
Bank accounts sustained by residents
Increased credit scores among residents
Decreased reliance on public assistance
Individuals moving from deeply subsidized to affordable or affordable to market rate housing
Pardons/expungements received
Long-term Outcomes: Increased financial health and economic independence

Description:
This strategy supports Riverside youth and adults in accessing sustainable employment that enables wealth building. For youth and young adults, RISE provides distinct pathways for in-school and out-of-school youth to gain employment skills and work experience. Both programs are designed to provide an eight week scaffolded, evidence-based curriculum of skill building developed by program specialists using individual student skill assessments to help students develop career and educational goals. This is followed by at least four weeks of work experience through externships (for in-school youth) or certifications (such as GEDs, for out-of-school youth). 

Through EMPOWER, adults and families receive ongoing case management services to meet their employment, financial, and additional goals. Programming includes employment-related trainings, workshops, personal coaching, and referrals to needed workforce resources that address barriers to employment and support job retention.  All workforce development programs include assessments to measure skill gains, provide individualized service strategies addressing participants' specific barriers, and track employment outcomes and retention to ensure participants not only obtain jobs but maintain employment over time.
Output:
Enrollment in and completion of job skills training programs
Enrollment in and completion of durability training programs
Externships and job placements
Immediate Outcomes:
Increased access to job skills training and support
Increased job skills
Increased youth awareness of career pathways and in-demand jobs
Increased access to a subsidized work experience
Intermediate Outcomes:
Increased youth and young adults' participation in career pathways and in-demand jobs
Increased attainment of employment-related certificates and/or credentials
Increased attainment of jobs that support wealth building
Long-term Outcomes:
Increased employment and job retention
Increased wages
Increased job promotions and advancements

Description: This strategy provides step-by-step guidance for residents to transform business ideas into thriving enterprises through community partnerships. The program serves both aspiring entrepreneurs who need professional knowledge to develop comprehensive business strategies and existing business owners seeking to strengthen their operations and access new resources. Participants receive instruction on business plan fundamentals, market analysis, financial projections, marketing strategies, and legal structures, while connecting to resources including small business loans, technical assistance, and networking opportunities. By focusing on community-based business creation, the strategy builds local ownership of commercial enterprises that serve neighborhood needs and keep wealth circulating within the community.

Individuals enrolled in entrepreneurial programs
